steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: "The word 'robot'" is the subject — singular. The sentence describes a historical event (the word's first use in the 1920s), so the verb must be past tense.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement with tense
steps.content:
Rule: singular subject takes a singular verb. Past tense of "be" for singular is "was".
  • Choice A — "were": plural past tense — does not agree with the singular subject "The word 'robot'". ✗
  • Choice B — "were being": plural past progressive — still plural, still wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — "was": singular past tense of "be" — agrees with the singular subject and matches the historical event. ✓
  • Choice D — "have been": plural present perfect — plural "have" violates agreement, and present perfect doesn't fit a completed historical introduction. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
